examples.title: Basic this Pointer Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates basic use of this pointer for name disambiguation
examples.explanation: The this pointer distinguishes member variables (this->x) from parameters (x) when they have the same name. The arrow operator (->) dereferences this to access members.

examples.title: Function Chaining with *this
examples.explanation: Each setter returns a reference to *this (the current object). This enables chaining multiple method calls in a single statement, creating a fluent interface pattern.

examples.title: this in Operator Overloading
examples.description: Demonstrates using this as left operand in operator overloading
examples.explanation: In operator+=, 'this' automatically points to the left operand object (c1). The operator modifies the object pointed to by this and returns *this for potential chaining.

examples.title: this Type in Const vs Non-Const Functions
examples.description: Shows the difference in this pointer type based on function constness
examples.explanation: In non-const functions, this is 'ClassName*const' allowing modification. In const functions, this becomes 'const ClassName*const' preventing modification. Static functions have no this pointer at all.
